notes: Best TW 2020 acute crash/recovery sweep row. Non-technical weights predate later bear-crash production reductions.
Adopted for 2022-style open-high close-low bear/downtrend. Condition: 0050 close < MA240 and MA60 < MA240.
Current range-style adopted weights. Not the best sweep row; retained to match production strategy discussion.
Best TW 2021 sweep row; not adopted because it is more aggressive than current range philosophy.
Best TW bull sweep row. Large drawdown indicates need to inspect path before overfitting.
Best weight-only US 2022 row still lost to SPY; use defensive gates/exits instead of only changing weights.
US 2022 defensive gate/exit sweep best row; supports higher entry gates and fewer positions in bear markets.
Best US 2021 range/rotation sweep row.
Best US bull sweep row.
US bear_downtrend is defined in code but has not been isolated and fully backtested as a separate period.
Best 2020 COVID crash/recovery defensive row. More aggressive than the robust two-bear-regime setup.
Robust US bear setup selected because the same parameters also produced the best 2022 bear/downtrend defense result.
Adopted US bear/downtrend defensive setup. Weight-only optimization lost to SPY; gate, max positions, and exit rule drove the improvement.
Focused TW bear-defense refresh using current scoring and current crash exit. Some newer/delisted watchlist symbols had no Yahoo OHLCV and were skipped.
Rejected: borrowing the US-style market gate and position cap missed too much of the 2020 Taiwan crash/recovery rebound and lagged both the current rule and 0050.
Focused TW downtrend refresh using current scoring and current penalty-adjusted exit. Some newer/delisted watchlist symbols had no Yahoo OHLCV and were skipped.
Not adopted for core strategy: it reduced max drawdown by about 1.63 percentage points but gave up about 0.28 percentage points of excess return versus the current rule.
Adopted for US bear_crash UI/recommendation gate. Separating crash repair from long downtrend improved 2020 excess from the robust MA10/55% setup by about 5.35 percentage points.
Focused split refresh confirms the existing downtrend MA10/55% setup remains best among tested thresholds and gates.
Rejected: raising downtrend entry threshold to 65% or higher reduced trade count but missed too many recoveries; 65% and 70% tied at -20.43%, and 75%/80% were worse.

Rejected for strategy-performance adoption: removing the newly-above-all-moving-averages entry filter improved over SPY but did not beat the existing US bull best sweep, which returned 134.11% with 80.29% excess. This UI rule may still be useful for broader manual review.

Direct comparison using the previously best US bull weights but with score-only entries. It underperformed the same weights under the original newly-above-all entry rule by about 15.20 percentage points of active return.
Reference for broad TW score-only attention entry. It beat 0050 but underperformed the earlier newly-above-all best sweep for 2021 range.

Close-above-MA5 entry improved 2021 range active return by about 1.43 percentage points versus score-only, but still trailed the earlier newly-above-all best sweep by about 7.95 points. Not adopted on this evidence alone.
Reference for broad TW bull score-only attention entry. Return was essentially tied with the earlier newly-above-all best sweep but retained very large path drawdown.

Close-above-MA5 entry was effectively tied with score-only and the earlier newly-above-all best sweep in the 2025-2026 bull period; the active-return difference versus score-only was only about 0.00024 percentage points.

command: uv run python scripts/backtest_tw_bear_defense.py --focused --slope-sweep --slope-mode focused --focused-label current_rule --case tw_bear_crash_2020 --top 10 --output-csv results/backtests/tw_bear_crash_2020_current_rule_ma_slope_focused.csv
notes: Focused slope-quality sweep improved 2020 crash/recovery versus the current refresh. Best slope mix rewards mild MA5 up plus MA60/MA120 repair, without a market gate.

command: uv run python scripts/backtest_tw_bear_defense.py --focused --slope-sweep --slope-mode focused --focused-label current_rule --case tw_bear_downtrend_2022 --top 10 --output-csv results/backtests/tw_bear_downtrend_2022_current_rule_ma_slope_focused.csv
notes: Focused downtrend slope-quality sweep improved active return and excess versus current 2022 refresh. Best mix gives modest MA5-up credit and annual-MA repair credit; broader long-slope bundle reduced drawdown but returned less.
Adding MA60/MA120/MA240 slope quality to the prior 2021 best base weights beat the earlier 2021 best sweep. Short MA5/MA20 slope was not required in the top row.
Highest-return bull slope row beat prior active return but is rejected for adoption because the path drawdown reached -100%; do not use without fixing portfolio risk controls and position caps.
Reference: adding slope quality to default TW weights improved 2021 range versus default baseline but did not beat the prior 2021 best weights; use best-base slope result instead.

Rejected: ordinary newly-above-all MA-slope sweep did not beat the current-rule bear-defense refresh for 2020 crash/recovery; the focused break-prev5-low current-rule slope variant performed much better.

Valuation (PE/PB, TW+US) and US EPS-surprise added as DISPLAY-ONLY context, not scored. A historical scoring backtest was attempted but blocked: Finnhub free tier company_earnings returns only ~4 quarters, earnings_calendar is gated (no announcement dates), and no historical PE/PB series is available from free sources. Adopted path A: dashboard badges (PB<=1.2x=downside cushion, PE>=40x=double-kill risk, EPS-surprise sign) + point-in-time daily log data/valuation_snapshots.jsonl to accumulate a forward dataset for a future backtest. Revisit scoring weights once enough forward snapshots exist or a paid point-in-time fundamentals source is added.

US profitability-trend (Jeff 獲利性分析) added as DISPLAY-ONLY context, not scored. Pulls quarterly gross/operating/net margins (newest-first, up to 6 quarters) from yfinance quarterly_income_stmt and shows: (1) per-margin direction arrows (latest vs oldest in window, >=1.0pp = improve/deteriorate), (2) a 本業 vs 業外 divergence flag when |net_margin - operating_margin| >= 10pp (net>op = one-time/non-operating inflation -> sell-the-news risk; net<op = one-time charge masking healthy operating margin -> possible over-sell/錯殺). Verified live on 86 US watchlist symbols: data present for all (typically 4-5 usable quarters); real hits include NVDA all-three-margins improving, BA operating-loss-but-positive-net inflation, INTC operating-profit-but-deep-net-loss depression. NOT scored: same blocker as valuation_eps_surprise_display_only_path_a -- yfinance margins are as-of-now, not historical point-in-time, so no clean backtest. Latest-quarter margins are logged to data/valuation_snapshots.jsonl (margin_period/gross_margin/operating_margin/net_margin) to build the forward dataset. Intended use is as confirmation alongside the technical breakout, not standalone.

US 領先財報佈局 (Jeff 內訓-3) added as DISPLAY-ONLY context, not scored. Zero extra fetches: revenue series reuses the same yfinance quarterly_income_stmt as the margin trend (Total Revenue, newest-first up to 8 quarters); EPS actuals reuse the same Finnhub company_earnings payload as the EPS surprise (actual field, newest-first). Dashboard shows: (1) 營收 YoY for the latest quarter when >=5 quarters available, flagged 由負翻正 when prior-year-quarter YoY was negative and latest is non-negative; (2) 營收/EPS 落底回升 flag when the single-quarter series is bottoming and turning up (latest > prior, latest != window-min, latest < window-max -> an early 領先 entry, not a chased peak); (3) sell-the-news caution when EPS or 營收 prints a multi-quarter high AND price is >=20% above MA20 (乖離 = close/ma20-1), the deck's exit pattern. NOT scored: same blocker as the other display-only fundamentals -- revenue/EPS levels from these sources are as-of-now snapshots, not survivorship-clean historical point-in-time series, so no clean backtest yet. revenues/eps_actuals live only in the latest_signals fundamental blob (display), they are intentionally NOT added to valuation_snapshots.jsonl this round to avoid churning the snapshot schema; the TW monthly-revenue point-in-time backtest (the genuinely backtestable path) is deferred. Intended use is confirmation alongside the technical breakout: 落底回升 + breakout = 領先佈局 buy; multi-quarter high + extended price = sell-the-news exit.

entry_rule: newly 站上全均線 (close>MA5/MA10/MA20/MA240, not all-above prior day) + point-in-time latest monthly-revenue YoY filter
exit_rule: event study: fwd 20/60 trading-day return, no portfolio/exit
source: scripts/fetch_tw_month_revenue.py (FinMind TaiwanStockMonthRevenue, anonymous), scripts/backtest_tw_revenue_yoy.py; events CSV results/backtests/tw_revenue_yoy_breakout_events.csv; commit e5299ff
notes: PURPOSE: test whether TW monthly-revenue YoY (the only genuinely point-in-time-historical free fundamental) adds value as a SCORING gate at a technical breakout. DATA BLOCKER BROKEN: FinMind gives full monthly-revenue history free/anonymous for all 231 TW watchlist codes; point-in-time enforced by lagging each row +10 days (revenue for month M public by 10th of M+1; FinMind dates the row at first-of-M+1). RESULT (excess vs 0050, 24102 breakout events, 212 symbols): ALL breakouts ex20 +0.57%/ex60 +1.98%, win~46%. YoY>0 (n=16730) ex60 +2.19% vs YoY<=0 (n=7372) ex60 +1.49% -- only ~0.7pp gap, ex20 essentially identical, win-rate ~equal. '翻正/turned positive' (n=2534) is WORSE: ex20 +0.32%, win 44.0% (likely already priced by the lagged public print). Binary YoY-sign edge is REGIME-INCONSISTENT per year (2020 +3.80pp, 2018/2024 help; 2019 -0.43pp, 2022 -1.36pp). Only HIGH-MAGNITUDE growth separates: YoY>=30% ex60 +3.51%, YoY>=50% +3.87%, but win-rate stays flat ~46-47% (tail-driven, i.e. a revenue-momentum effect largely collinear with the existing 站上全均線/相對強度 momentum rules). VERDICT: do NOT add monthly-revenue YoY as a scored gate -- the binary turn is not robust, '翻正' is negative, and the magnitude edge overlaps existing momentum. Keep fundamentals DISPLAY-ONLY (now evidence-backed, not just a data-availability assumption). Caveats: survivorship (current watchlist), unconditional event study (no portfolio/position-sizing), only the newly-above-all-MAs entry, 20/60d horizons (deck thesis is multi-quarter), mechanical YoY cannot capture the qualitative 'why revenue turns' the deck centers on. Optional follow-up: a display-only TW 營收 YoY / high-growth badge using the same FinMind fetcher (parallels the US revenue display), still not scored.

entry_rule: newly_above_all_mas_and_score_ratio_gte_50pct (DEFAULT_WEIGHTS; backtest entry scoring drifted -- target signal inactive after AnalystSnapshot refactor)
notes: 3-way exit comparison, SAME entry signals. Hard MA5 exit (current adopted bull rule) excess +54.32%; graded penalty NO ma5 term (ma10=0.08) thr10 excess +65.10%; graded penalty V2 (ma5=0.03, ma10=0.15) thr10 excess +69.96% AND lowest DD -23.38%. FINDING: biggest lever is switching hard-MA5->graded-penalty (+11pp); adding soft MA5 term +1.7pp; raising ma10 to 0.15 a further +3.1pp. thr10 (patient) beats thr20 (+57%) in bull. CAVEAT: backtest scripts drifted from screener API (AnalystSnapshot target refactor); only the fix to a crashing kwarg was applied, so target-raise entry signal never fires -> absolute returns NOT comparable to older registry rows, but variant-vs-variant is apples-to-apples (identical entries). Not yet ported to production; scoring uses hard-MA5 in bull today.

SAME entry signals. Hard MA5 exit excess -0.15% (basically matches 0050); graded penalty at thr20: NO ma5 +11.46%, V2 +11.62% (adding MA5 ~neutral at thr20, +0.16pp). At thr10 adding MA5 helps more (no_ma5 +3.84% -> V2 +7.84%), but thr20 dominates thr10 in range. FINDING: switching hard-MA5->graded-penalty is a huge win in range (+11.7pp); MA5 term marginal at the winning threshold. Same drift caveat as bull row.

2020 acute crash/recovery. SAME entry signals. Hard MA5 exit excess -4.34%; graded penalty thr20: no_ma5 +0.11%, ma5=0.05/ma10=0.15 +0.53%, V2(ma5=0.03) -0.24%. Graded beats hard-MA5 by ~+5pp; MA5-term effect small and threshold-sensitive. Note the dedicated bear_crash defensive rule (break_prev5_low / big_bull_low) is a separate, better-suited exit for this regime; this row only isolates the MA5-penalty question. Same drift caveat.

SAME entry signals. Hard MA5 exit excess +53.16%; graded penalty thr10: no_ma5 +70.73%, ma5only(ma5=0.03/ma10=0.08) +71.83% (BEST), V2(ma5=0.03/ma10=0.15) +68.24%. FINDING: graded>>hard-MA5 (+18pp); adding soft MA5 +1.1pp; UNLIKE TW, raising ma10 to 0.15 HURTS US bull (-2.5pp vs ma5only). So the heavier-MA10 leg is TW-friendly, US-neutral-to-negative -> a per-market penalty table may be warranted. Same drift caveat.

SAME entry signals. All variants TRAIL SPY (negative excess) in 2021 US range. Hard MA5 exit -2.90%; graded penalty thr20: no_ma5 -3.54%, V2 -0.33% (BEST, least bad). Adding MA5 term helps +3.2pp here and beats hard-MA5, but the whole regime underperforms buy-and-hold SPY. Same drift caveat.

SAME entry signals. All variants lose in 2022 US downtrend (as expected -- this regime should use the adopted break_big_bull_low+vol defensive exit, not a graded-MA penalty). Hard MA5 -11.13%; graded thr20: no_ma5 -11.13%, V2 -10.45% (adding MA5 trims loss +0.68pp). Confirms MA5-penalty is not a bear tool; recorded for completeness. Same drift caveat.

entry_rule: newly_above_all_mas_and_score_ratio_gte_50pct (DEFAULT_WEIGHTS; entry scoring drifted -- target signal inactive)
exit_rule: penalty_adjusted_score_lt_10pct with soft below_ma5=0.03 + below_ma10=0.08 (ma5only, OOS candidate)
notes: OUT-OF-SAMPLE bull (independent of 2025-2026). Low-dispersion TW bull (all variants clustered 26-35% vs 0050 +32.9%). Excess: hard-MA5 +1.05%, no_ma5_thr10 +1.41% (best), ma5only_thr10 +0.70% (this row), V2 +0.28%, ma5_05/ma10_15 -1.66%. VERDICT for TW 2019: graded ~= hard-MA5 (within noise); adding MA5 term HURTS here; heavy MA10 (V2/ma5_05) clearly worst. thr20 all ~ -5%. Contradicts the large TW 2025-2026 graded advantage -> that was sample-favourable. Same harness-drift caveat (relative comparison valid).

entry_rule: newly_above_all_mas_and_score_ratio_gte_50pct (DEFAULT_WEIGHTS; entry scoring drifted -- target signal inactive)
exit_rule: penalty_adjusted_score_lt_10pct with soft below_ma5=0.03 + below_ma10=0.08 (ma5only, OOS candidate)
notes: OUT-OF-SAMPLE bull. Excess vs 0050 +90.6%: ma5only_thr10 +35.07% (BEST, this row), hard-MA5 +34.82%, V2 +34.18%, no_ma5_thr10 +30.72%. VERDICT for TW 2023-24: graded ~= hard-MA5 (+0.25pp, tie); but adding MA5 term helps a lot here (ma5only +4.3pp over no_ma5); heavy MA10 (V2) worse than ma5only. Combined with TW 2019, TW graded-vs-hard is a WASH on return; MA5-term inconsistent (hurts 2019, helps 2023-24). Same drift caveat.

entry_rule: newly_above_all_mas_and_score_ratio_gte_50pct (DEFAULT_WEIGHTS; entry scoring drifted -- target signal inactive)
exit_rule: penalty_adjusted_score_lt_10pct with soft below_ma5=0.03 + below_ma10=0.08 (ma5only, OOS candidate)
notes: OUT-OF-SAMPLE bull. Excess vs SPY +33.3%: ma5only_thr10 +2.83% (BEST, this row), no_ma5_thr10 +1.71%, V2 +1.20%, hard-MA5 +0.93%. VERDICT for US 2019: graded beats hard-MA5 +1.9pp; adding MA5 helps +1.1pp; heavy MA10 (V2) worse than ma5only. Consistent with US 2025-2026. Same drift caveat.

entry_rule: newly_above_all_mas_and_score_ratio_gte_50pct (DEFAULT_WEIGHTS; entry scoring drifted -- target signal inactive)
exit_rule: penalty_adjusted_score_lt_10pct with soft below_ma5=0.03 + below_ma10=0.08 (ma5only, OOS candidate)
notes: OUT-OF-SAMPLE bull. Excess vs SPY +56.8%: ma5only_thr10 +29.77% (BEST, this row), V2_thr20 +29.29%, ma5only_thr20 +28.89%, V2_thr10 +28.52%, no_ma5_thr10 +27.60%, hard-MA5 +23.92%. VERDICT for US 2023-24: graded beats hard-MA5 by +5.9pp; adding MA5 +2.2pp over no_ma5; ma5only(MA10=0.08) beats V2(MA10=0.15). OVERALL US: ma5only_thr10 is OOS-robust and clearly beats hard-MA5 in both windows. Same drift caveat.

entry_rule: exposure model: base=1.0 if regime in {bull,range} else 0.70 (regime per market_regime.py rules)
exit_rule: gap overlay (leading): early de-risk 1.0->0.70 on unfilled index down-gap OR fast-filled up-gap; early re-risk 0.70->1.0 on fresh vol-confirmed unfilled up-gap; next-day, 0.1% switch fee
notes: PURPOSE: test whether daily gap signals on the index (0050.TW) add value as a LEADING correction layer on top of the existing regime 100/70 exposure throttle. RESULT (min_gap=0.5%): buy_hold total +587.38% maxDD -33.83%; regime (100/70) +524.55% maxDD -28.01% (30 switches); regime_gap +469.92% maxDD -26.86% (167 switches). Gap overlay REJECTED: across min_gap sweep 0.15/0.3/0.5/1.0%, regime_gap always underperforms plain regime on total return and only marginally improves maxDD; as threshold rises to clean-gap level (1%) regime_gap converges to regime (517 vs 525, maxDD -26.6 vs -28.0) i.e. no robust incremental edge. Bear-period return looks better (-17% vs -24%) but that is just being out of the market more, not gap alpha. early_rerisk (gaps to re-enter bull early) essentially never fires usefully (57 days, mostly ineffective) — index up-gaps in bear are too rare. INCIDENTAL POSITIVE: the plain regime 100/70 water-level (no gaps) cuts maxDD ~5.8pp (-33.8->-28.0) for only ~63pp total-return give-up over 8yr — worth pursuing as its own idea. CAVEATS: single 2018-2026 path; 0050 adjusted close (total-return proxy, ex-div gaps removed by adjustment); regime classified on adjusted vs raw in prod (minor). Does NOT condemn stock-level gaps (Exp 2): single stocks gap far more often/meaningfully than an index; test gaps there as a scoring/sell-pressure signal, not exposure timing. min_gap also swept at 0.0015/0.003/0.01 (see notes above / known-results).

entry_rule: exposure model: base=1.0 if regime in {bull,range} else 0.70 (regime per market_regime.py rules)
exit_rule: gap overlay (leading): early de-risk 1.0->0.70 on unfilled index down-gap OR fast-filled up-gap; early re-risk 0.70->1.0 on fresh vol-confirmed unfilled up-gap; next-day, 0.1% switch fee
notes: PURPOSE: same as TW row, on SPY. RESULT (min_gap=0.5%): buy_hold +216.49% maxDD -33.72%; regime (100/70) +199.33% maxDD -28.35% (32 switches); regime_gap +178.13% maxDD -28.35% (71 switches). Gap overlay REJECTED: regime_gap adds NO maxDD improvement over plain regime (identical -28.35%) and costs ~21pp total return; at clean 1% threshold regime_gap == regime almost exactly (200.45 vs 199.33). US index gaps are even rarer than TW (40 up / 27 down over 8yr at 0.5%), early_rerisk fires only 10 days. INCIDENTAL POSITIVE: plain regime 100/70 cuts maxDD 5.4pp (-33.7->-28.4) for ~17pp total-return give-up. Same caveats as TW row. Stock-level (Exp 2) still worth testing separately. min_gap also swept at 0.0015/0.003/0.01 (see notes above / known-results).

entry_rule: event study: daily true gaps (up: low>prev_high*(1+1%); down: high<prev_low*(1-1%)) on adjusted prices; ex-div removed by adjustment
exit_rule: event study: fwd 10/20/60d excess vs benchmark, plus post-observation excess from t+10; no portfolio
compared_against: beta-neutral all-day baseline of same watchlist
notes: PURPOSE: before wiring gaps into score.py, test if watchlist gap signals predict fwd excess. CRITICAL CONTROL: down-gaps ALSO showed +excess -> excess-vs-benchmark is contaminated by high-beta watchlist selection; must judge vs baseline(all-day mean excess) ex10=+0.19% ex20=+0.41% ex60=+1.50%. FINDINGS (n=5839 up, 5787 down): UP gap ex60 +6.87% (=+5.4pp over baseline) real short-term momentum, but win~50% (tail-driven) and largely COLLINEAR with existing new_high/above_all/relative_strength momentum rules (same overlap that got monthly-revenue-YoY rejected). VOLUME CONFIRMATION HURTS: UP vol-confirmed ex60 +4.85% < plain +6.87% -> refutes '帶量突破更強' here. 10-DAY OBSERVATION rule (post-obs from t+10): unfilled +2.41% vs filled +1.14% = modest ~1.3pp TW edge (US null, see us row). KEEPER SIGNAL: UP fast-filled (<=2 bars) ex10 -1.53% win 37.4% = -1.7pp BELOW baseline, sign-flips vs everything else, ORTHOGONAL to existing sell-pressure levels (references the gap-origin high, not MA/prev-low) -> candidate 賣壓扣分 rule '向上跳空2日內回補=假突破'. Caveat: fast_filled known by t+2 overlaps first 2d of the 10d window (partly mechanical); effect fades by 60d (ex60 +0.83% vs baseline +1.50%, i.e. -0.7pp) -> use as SHORT-TERM avoidance/exit only. DOWN gaps NOT cleanly bearish (mild mean-reversion +excess at 60d) -> no short rule. VERDICT: carry ONLY the fast-fill penalty to Exp 2b portfolio backtest; bullish up-gap rule likely redundant with existing momentum; drop volume gating and down-gap penalty; 10-day rule TW-only. Caveats: survivorship (current watchlist), unconditional event study (no portfolio/position-sizing), adjusted-close.

entry_rule: event study: daily true gaps (up: low>prev_high*(1+1%); down: high<prev_low*(1-1%)) on adjusted prices; ex-div removed by adjustment
exit_rule: event study: fwd 10/20/60d excess vs benchmark, plus post-observation excess from t+10; no portfolio
compared_against: beta-neutral all-day baseline of same watchlist
notes: PURPOSE: same as tw row, SPY benchmark. CONTROL baseline: baseline(all-day mean excess) ex10=+0.70% ex20=+1.46% ex60=+4.46% (US watchlist beats SPY strongly -> even bigger contamination). FINDINGS (n=2685 up, 2254 down): UP gap ex60 +7.72% (=+3.3pp over baseline), UP breakaway(new 20d high) ex60 +8.51% win 56.7% best subset (=+4.1pp over baseline). VOLUME CONFIRMATION HURTS here too (vol ex60 +6.18% < plain +7.72%). 10-DAY OBSERVATION rule is NULL in US: unfilled post20 +2.48% vs filled +2.49% identical -> the '10日未回補=強' rule does NOT generalize to US. KEEPER SIGNAL confirmed cross-market: UP fast-filled ex10 -2.33% win 34.9% = -3.0pp BELOW baseline (fades to -0.5pp by 60d). VERDICT (cross-market): only fast-fill penalty is robust+orthogonal; bullish up-gap collinear with momentum; drop volume gating; 10-day rule TW-only not US. Same caveats as tw row.

entry_rule: newly_above_all_mas_and_score_ratio_gte_50pct (DEFAULT_WEIGHTS; harness signal-drift as in other exit-sweep rows -> variant-vs-variant valid, absolute returns not comparable to older rows)
exit_rule: penalty_adjusted_score_lt_20pct OR fast-filled up-gap (up-gap filled <=2 bars, min_gap 1%); next open
compared_against: same baseline exit without fast-fill overlay (penalty_score_lt_20pct and baseline_ma5)
notes: Exp 2b — fast-fill up-gap exit overlay. Numeric fields are the penalty_score_lt_20pct+fastfill variant. Δexcess vs its baseline: penalty+ff +0.64pp; ma5+ff +2.96pp (helps (small)). VERDICT REJECTED as blanket exit — regime-inconsistent (small positive in bear, negative/mixed elsewhere), overlaps existing MA5/penalty exits so most added exits are noise. See sibling rows for other regimes; only a BEAR-GATED variant is consistently positive across TW+US bear but rests on 2 bear windows (overfit risk, not adopted). fastfill_only is degenerate (~130-160d holds ≈ buy&hold). Caveats: survivorship (current watchlist; 5289.TW dropped; US recent-IPO gaps CRWV/ARM/ALAB/GEV/SNDK missing early), harness signal-drift, fast_filled known by t+2 partly mechanical.

exit_rule: penalty_adjusted_score_lt_20pct OR fast-filled up-gap (up-gap filled <=2 bars, min_gap 1%); next open
compared_against: same baseline exit without fast-fill overlay (penalty_score_lt_20pct and baseline_ma5)
notes: Exp 2b — fast-fill up-gap exit overlay. Numeric fields are the penalty_score_lt_20pct+fastfill variant. Δexcess vs its baseline: penalty+ff +0.53pp; ma5+ff +0.51pp (helps (small)). VERDICT REJECTED as blanket exit — regime-inconsistent (small positive in bear, negative/mixed elsewhere), overlaps existing MA5/penalty exits so most added exits are noise. See sibling rows for other regimes; only a BEAR-GATED variant is consistently positive across TW+US bear but rests on 2 bear windows (overfit risk, not adopted). fastfill_only is degenerate (~130-160d holds ≈ buy&hold). Caveats: survivorship (current watchlist; 5289.TW dropped; US recent-IPO gaps CRWV/ARM/ALAB/GEV/SNDK missing early), harness signal-drift, fast_filled known by t+2 partly mechanical.

Caveats: survivorship (current watchlist; 5289.TW dropped; US recent-IPO gaps CRWV/ARM/ALAB/GEV/SNDK missing early), harness signal-drift, fast_filled known by t+2 partly mechanical.

entry_rule: regime-gated NEW entries only (no force-sell): held stocks exit only on close<MA5; pure {stocks+cash} book, NO benchmark parking; numeric fields = no_add_bear (stop entries only in bear)
exit_rule: close<MA5 next open, always active every regime (unchanged across modes)
compared_against: baseline (enter all regimes, same stocks+cash book) and 0050 buy-hold
notes: PURPOSE: user-refined idea — in a weak regime only STOP BUYING; held stocks NOT force-sold, they exit on close<MA5 and freed cash idles (pure stocks+cash, no index parking). Full-cycle TW (total% / maxDD% / buys / avg_cash%): buy_hold 591.15 / -33.83; baseline 409.42 / -44.31 / 11159 / 39.6%; no_add_bear 375.11 / -49.43 / 9561 / 46.6%; no_add_range_bear 194.73 / -71.95 / 4412 / 68.1%. VERDICT REJECTED for TW: gating still HURTS both return AND drawdown (baseline -44.3% -> no_add_bear -49.4% -> no_add_range_bear -72.0%); lagging regime means paused entries don't avoid the in-regime correction, and a concentrated book with no index cushion draws down hard. SECOND, BIGGER FINDING: this pure stocks+cash book is far worse than the production harness that PARKS idle cash in the index (cf. earlier force-sell run where TW baseline WITH parking was ~+1087% / -29.6% vs this no-parking baseline +409% / -44.3%); holding ~40% avg cash instead of the index costs hundreds of pp return AND worsens drawdown. Lesson: keep idle capital in the index, not cash. Caveats: single path; yfinance adjusted-close is mildly non-deterministic run-to-run (~1%, does not change conclusions); survivorship (5289.TW dropped); trade counts now tracked. Supersedes force-sell variant (CSV overwritten).

entry_rule: regime-gated NEW entries only (no force-sell): held stocks exit only on close<MA5; pure {stocks+cash} book, NO benchmark parking; numeric fields = no_add_bear (stop entries only in bear)
exit_rule: close<MA5 next open, always active every regime (unchanged across modes)
compared_against: baseline (enter all regimes, same stocks+cash book) and SPY buy-hold
notes: PURPOSE: same as tw row, SPY. Full-cycle US (total% / maxDD% / buys / avg_cash%): buy_hold 217.59 / -33.72; baseline 203.43 / -20.99 / 6903 / 52.1%; no_add_bear 200.97 / -15.25 / 6346 / 55.9%; no_add_range_bear 105.90 / -10.14 / 3849 / 71.9%. NUANCE: unlike TW, US no_add_bear is NEAR-FREE drawdown protection (200.97 vs baseline 203.43, i.e. -2.5pp return for -5.7pp less DD -20.99->-15.25); no_add_range_bear cuts DD to -10.1% but halves return (106 vs 203). So pausing entries in BEAR ONLY is mildly attractive for US, but the cross-market inconsistency (TW hurts, US helps) makes it non-robust. SAME BIG FINDING: pure stocks+cash worse than index-parking; ~52% avg cash is a large drag. Caveats: single path; yfinance adjusted-close mildly non-deterministic (~1%); US survivorship + recent-IPO gaps; trade counts now tracked. Supersedes force-sell variant.
